2604.06820.title: Beyond Surface Judgments: Human-Grounded Risk Evaluation of LLM-Generated Disinformation
2604.06820.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) can generate persuasive narratives at scale, raising concerns about their potential use in disinformation campaigns. Assessing this risk ultimately requires understanding how readers receive such content. In practice, however, LLM judges are increasingly used as a low-cost substitute for direct human evaluation, even though whether they faithfully track reader responses remains unclear. We recast evaluation in this setting as a proxy-validity problem and audit LLM judges against human reader responses. Using 290 aligned articles, 2,043 paired human ratings, and outputs from eight frontier judges, we examine judge--human alignment in terms of overall scoring, item-level ordering, and signal dependence. We find persistent judge--human gaps throughout. Relative to humans, judges are typically harsher, recover item-level human rankings only weakly, and rely on different textual signals, placing more weight on logical rigour while penalizing emotional intensity more strongly. At the same time, judges agree far more with one another than with human readers. These results suggest that LLM judges form a coherent evaluative group that is much more aligned internally than it is with human readers, indicating that internal agreement is not evidence of validity as a proxy for reader response.
2604.06820.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）能夠大規模生成具有說服力的敘述，這引發了對其在虛假信息活動中潛在使用的擔憂。評估這一風險最終需要理解讀者如何接收這類內容。然而，在實踐中，LLM 評估者越來越多地被用作直接人類評估的低成本替代品，儘管它們是否忠實地反映讀者反應仍不明確。我們將這一設定中的評估重新構建為一個代理有效性問題，並對 LLM 評估者與人類讀者反應進行審核。使用 290 篇對齊的文章、2,043 組配對的人類評分和來自八位前沿評估者的輸出，我們檢查評估者與人類在整體評分、項目級排序和信號依賴性方面的對齊情況。我們發現評估者與人類之間存在持續的差距。相對於人類，評估者通常更嚴厲，僅弱地恢復項目級的人類排名，並依賴不同的文本信號，對邏輯嚴謹性賦予更多權重，同時對情感強度的懲罰更強。與此同時，評估者之間的共識遠高於與人類讀者之間的共識。這些結果表明，LLM 評估者形成了一個內部一致的評估群體，其內部對齊程度遠高於與人類讀者的對齊程度，這表明內部一致性並不是作為讀者反應代理的有效性證據。
2604.06820.author: Zonghuan Xu et.al.
2604.06820.authors: Zonghuan Xu, Xiang Zheng, Yutao Wu, Xingjun Ma
